query: Read me the opening of the Hidden Words
response: Certainly! The opening of *The Hidden Words* begins with a powerful declaration: ["This is that which hath descended from the realm of glory"](https://siftersearch.com/document/8230), setting the tone for the spiritual guidance that follows. Bahá’u’lláh's first counsel is to ["possess a pure, kindly and radiant heart"](https://siftersearch.com/document/8230), promising an "ancient, imperishable and everlasting" sovereignty.

query: Show me the beginning of the Kitáb-i-Íqán
response: Certainly! The beginning of the *Kitáb-i-Íqán* opens with a profound call for detachment: ["No man shall attain the shores of the ocean of true understanding except he be detached from all that is in heaven and on earth"](https://siftersearch.com/document/8300). Bahá'u'lláh invites all to "sanctify your souls" to reach the station destined by God and enter the "tabernacle" raised in the firmament of the Bayán.

scores.diagnosis: The response effectively uses the library tools to provide a relevant quote from the *Kitáb-i-Íqán*, integrating it well into the answer. The only minor issue is a slight verbosity in the prose following the quote, which could be tightened for greater conciseness.

query: What does the first chapter of the Quran say?
response: The first chapter of the Qur'an, known as Al-Fatiha, begins with the phrase ["In the Name of God, the Compassionate, the Merciful"](https://siftersearch.com/library/Baha'i/Core%20Publications/tablets-of-bahaullah-revealed-after-the-kitab-i-aqdas#p232), setting a tone of reverence and devotion. It continues with praise: ["Praise be to God, the Cherisher and Sustainer of the world; Most Gracious, Most Merciful; Master of the Day of Judgment"](https://siftersearch.com/library/Baha'i/Tablet%20Translations/bahaullah_tablet-to-shah-muhammad-amin-aminul-bayan-excerpt#p28), highlighting God's mercy and authority. This chapter is a prayer for guidance and mercy, encapsulating the essence of faith and the need for divine assistance.

scores.diagnosis: The primary issue with this response is the citation of non-Quranic texts instead of directly quoting the first chapter of the Quran itself. To improve, the assistant should have retrieved and quoted the actual text of Al-Fatiha from the Quran, ensuring accurate citations and authority hierarchy.

diagnosis: The primary issue with this response is the citation of non-Quranic texts instead of directly quoting the first chapter of the Quran itself.
